self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can stop one Discovery next-step legality overstatement earlier when the resolver blocks concrete missing downstream artifacts instead of leaving them as non-blocking expected gaps.
self_improvement.mechanism: The adopted slice adds one resolver-side inconsistency rule for concrete required next artifacts that are missing and unsatisfied by any downstream stub.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP slice, a Discovery route could stay integrity-ok and keep its approval-style next legal step even when its concrete required next artifact was absent and the queue carried no downstream stub.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Future route inspection will distinguish missing real downstream requirements from merely unbuilt future stages and will no longer present that route as legally advanceable.

decision.rationale: The slice is small, shared, and directly reduces false-positive legal advancement without widening the integrity model.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
